question.text: An investor purchased equity mutual fund units for Rs. 5,00,000 and redeemed them after 14 months for Rs. 7,00,000. What is the total tax liability including cess under Section 112A?

answer.text: Rs. 9,750 (12.5% on Rs. 75,000 after Rs. 1.25 lakh exemption + 4% cess)
Rs. 26,000 (12.5% on full Rs. 2,00,000 gain + 4% cess)

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Holding period >12 months for equity MF = LTCG. Gain = Rs. 7,00,000 - 5,00,000 = Rs. 2,00,000. Under Section 112A, LTCG up to Rs. 1,25,000 per year is exempt. Taxable LTCG = 2,00,000 - 1,25,000 = Rs. 75,000. Tax = 75,000 x 12.5% = Rs. 9,375. With 4% cess = 9,375 + 375 = Rs. 9,750.
**The Trap:** Option A uses the old 10% LTCG rate (pre-Budget 2024). Option C forgets the Rs. 1.25 lakh exemption entirely. Option D incorrectly treats this as STCG despite holding >12 months.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Budget 2024 changed equity LTCG rate from 10% to 12.5% and exemption from Rs. 1 lakh to Rs. 1.25 lakh. Both changes effective from July 23, 2024.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always remind clients that the Rs. 1.25 lakh LTCG exemption is per financial year across ALL equity-oriented assets (shares + equity MFs combined), not per scheme.
